fluid ( injection fluid ), which forms a kind of “ sleeve ”, each fluid maintaining its relative position until reaching the helical tube ( 3 ). in this tube ( 3 ), part of the fluids varies its speed along the bends , slowing down on the longer bends in relation to the other part of the fluids , which travels more quickly and with force towards the centre of tube ( 3 ) ( centripetal force ) on the shorter bends , thereby causing the dragging of the suction fluid , which is compressed by the transporting fluid thus causing the two fluids to totally mix , converting the centrifugal force and helical movement of the fluids at the inlet into force and rectilinear movement at the outlet of the said helical tube ( 3 ). this is the ideal way to carry out extraction with neutralisation of pollutants coming , for example , from chimneys . the most significant example has as a transporting fluid water injected into tube ( 6 ) by means of a pump ( not shown ) and as a fluid to be dragged a gaseous fluid possibly loaded with pollutant elements . b ) injection through the inside of the injection tube ( 1 )— fig2 . the transporting fluid is compressed inside the injection tube and when it expands inside the decompressor ( 2 ) it forms an expansion cone which depends on the injection pressure , intercepting the suction fluid in the rectilinear part of the decompressor ( 2 ). this depends on the angle of the decompressor ( 2 ) and on the injection pressure of the transporting fluid . the force and rectilinear movement of the transporting fluid cause the dragging of the suction fluid which frictionally mixes with the first fluid ( injection fluid ) due to the centrifugal force and helical movement created on the outside of the injection tube ( 1 ) inside this suction fluid . this conflict between the forces and movements of the two fluids facilitates possible chemical reactions between the fluids and / or particles . it is the ideal way to naturally oxygenate water by means of forced aeration inside the apparatus . the most significant example uses water as a transporting fluid injected by means of a pump ( not shown ) into tube ( 5 ) and injection tube ( 1 ), and atmospheric air as a second fluid to be dragged and available through tube ( 6 ) and the outside of the injection tube ( 1 ), these fluids mixing intimately inside the rectilinear part of the decompressor ( 2 ), providing excellent oxygenation of water , for example swimming pool water . the flow of the suction fluid increases with the flow of the injection fluid and the two increase with the increase in injection pressure . the fluid mixing device is a technically simple piece of equipment that effectively resolves environmental problems . the use of the characteristics of extraction with the total mixing of the suction elements by the transporting fluid makes the equipment effective in the chemical neutralization of air , together with the extraction of the pollution of a chimney . the use of the characteristics of suction with the conflict between the force and movement of the two fluids makes the equipment ideal for aerating water and effluents . the method is efficient in the oxidation of nutrients existing in water ( grease , iron , nitrates , etc .) and in the aerobic respiration of bacteria in effluents due to the high klav content . as aeration occurs inside the apparatus , this avoids any environmental impact in the case of the aeration of effluents . the characteristics of high flow rate and suction force make the apparatus an alternative to its use as a vacuum pump . the characteristics of compression and expansion of the transporting fluid with centrifugal force make it possible to directly transfer heat from one fluid to the other .']

from functools import reduce
shortest_word = rdd.flatMap(lambda line: line.split()) \
               .reduce(lambda x,y : x if len(x) < len (y) else y)
print(f"the shortest word is :{shortest_word}")

the shortest word is :.
In [22]:
import requests

dataset = 'ccdv/patent-classification'
HUGGING_FACE_PARQUET_API = 'https://huggingface.co/api/datasets/{dataset}/parquet'

r = requests.get(HUGGING_FACE_PARQUET_API.format(dataset=dataset))
print(f"Status Code: {r.status_code}")
print(f"JSON Response: {r.json()}")
train_parquet_files = r.json()['patent']['train']

Status Code: 200
JSON Response: {'patent': {'test': ['https://huggingface.co/api/datasets/ccdv/patent-classification/parquet/patent/test/0.parquet'], 'train': ['https://huggingface.co/api/datasets/ccdv/patent-classification/parquet/patent/train/0.parquet'], 'validation': ['https://huggingface.co/api/datasets/ccdv/patent-classification/parquet/patent/validation/0.parquet']}, 'abstract': {'test': ['https://huggingface.co/api/datasets/ccdv/patent-classification/parquet/abstract/test/0.parquet'], 'train': ['https://huggingface.co/api/datasets/ccdv/patent-classification/parquet/abstract/train/0.parquet'], 'validation': ['https://huggingface.co/api/datasets/ccdv/patent-classification/parquet/abstract/validation/0.parquet']}}

        .appName('load parquet file to pyspark DF') \
        .getOrCreate()
In [24]:
from pyspark import SparkFiles

for url in train_parquet_files:
  spark.sparkContext.addFile(url)

  df = spark.read.parquet(SparkFiles.getRootDirectory() + "/*.parquet")
  df.show()

In [3]:
from pyspark.sql import functions as F


df_selected = df.select("text", "label")


print("\n--- Null Counts ---")
df_selected.select([F.count(F.when(F.col(c).isNull(), c)).alias(c + "_nulls") for c in df_selected.columns]).show()


print("\n--- Summary Statistics ---")
df_selected.describe().show()


print("\n--- Quantiles for 'label' ---")
if 'label' in df_selected.columns and str(df_selected.schema['label'].dataType) in ['LongType', 'IntegerType']:
    quantiles = df_selected.approxQuantile("label", [0.25, 0.5, 0.75], 0.01)
    print("Quantiles for label:", quantiles)
else:
    print("Label column is not numeric, skipping quantiles.")


print("\n--- Skewness and Kurtosis for 'label' ---")
if 'label' in df_selected.columns and str(df_selected.schema['label'].dataType) in ['LongType', 'IntegerType']:
    df_selected.select(
        F.skewness("label").alias("skewness_label"),
        F.kurtosis("label").alias("kurtosis_label")
    ).show()
else:
    print("Label column is not numeric, skipping skewness and kurtosis.")


print("\n--- Mode of 'text' ---")
df_selected.groupBy("text").count().orderBy(F.desc("count")).show(1, truncate=False)

print("\n--- Mode of 'label' ---")
df_selected.groupBy("label").count().orderBy(F.desc("count")).show(1)


print("\n--- IPC Classes (Not applicable with current DataFrame schema) ---")
print("The current DataFrame does not contain 'ipc_classes' column.")

+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----+
|the invention is illustrated by way of example and not by way of limitation in the figures of the accompanying drawings in which like references indicate similar elements . references to embodiments in this disclosure are not necessarily to the same embodiment , and such references mean at least one . while specific implementations are discussed , it is understood that this is done for illustrative purposes only . a person skilled in the relevant art will recognize that other components and configurations may be used without departing from the scope and spirit of the invention . in the following description , numerous specific details are set forth to provide a thorough description of the invention . however , it will be apparent to one skilled in the art that the invention may be practiced without these specific details . in other instances , well - known features have not been described in detail so as not to obscure the invention . an application can depend on multiple components within different projects and each of those components may also have dependencies on other components within other projects . each component &# 39 ; s project may in turn have dependencies on other components &# 39 ; projects . this chain might end with the runtime library for the target computing system . in various embodiments , a dependency graph provides a way to determine how projects are interrelated . a simplified diagram is shown in fig1 . in this figure , the main project depends on a data access library and a business logic component . these , in turn , depend upon a system runtime library . systems and methods in accordance with embodiments of the present disclosure overcome the problems described above by efficiently tracking the relationships between a project and changes to source code for components upon which the project &# 39 ; s application depends . it will be appreciated by those of skill in the relevant art that the embodiments of the present disclosure are not dependent on or limited by the particular programming language ( s ) found in the source code . in various embodiments , a project dependency data structure can represent the dependencies of projects on components . in aspects of these embodiments , this data structure is a directed acyclic graph ( dag ) formed by references between class path level nodes ( cpls ). cpls model the ordered dependencies of a set of projects and the individual components and files within the projects . cpls are coupled with a dependency resolution mechanism that ensures dependent projects reflect the latest versions of components . fig2 provides an exemplary illustration of a project dependency data structure in accordance to various embodiments . in various embodiments and by way of illustration , each project has a cpl 200 . a cpl can hold a list of source files associated with a project and one or more binary paths . a binary - path is an ordered list that can include references to jar files 202 , locations of binary ( e . g ., “. class ”) files 204 , and references to other cpls 206 . the order of elements in the binary path has significance , since it establishes a search priority . a reference to a cpl in a binary path indicates that a project depends on the code in another project ( source code or binary code ). the project dependency data structure provides optimizations for commonly used components . many applications and components may depend upon one or more commonly used components and in the absence of the present invention copies of commonly used components are often stored inefficiently with every project that uses the component . for example , most or all components depend on the runtime library of the target computing system and a copy of this library may be included with every component project . however , the software analysis system can utilize the project dependency data structure to maintain a single copy of each common component used across all the projects . this saves space and reduces the time required to build the projects . as described in the previous paragraph , different projects might refer to the same resource . in conventional software development environments , when several projects are loaded simultaneously , it is common for a development environment to create duplicate in - memory representations for each resource that was referred to by multiple projects . this increased the memory and cpu utilization of the compilation system . the project dependency data structure also allows a compilation or other system to understand the common dependencies across projects and load a single version of each shared resource . the project dependency data structure also serves as a hierarchy for specifying the resources a project depends on . in conventional software development environments , projects specified the resources they depended on with a flat list . in cases where project a depended on project b , which depended on project c , project a was required to specify all resources required by projects a , b and c in a single list . project b was required to specify all resources required by projects b and c in a single list . therefore , any resource required by project c had to be duplicated in the flat lists associated with projects a , b and c . as changes occur to these separate projects , keeping these lists synchronized could be a challenge . a common problem was for projects a , b and c to end up referring to different , incompatible versions of the same resource . in various embodiments , the software analysis system can locate resources available to the project ( e . g ., files , directories , data types , etc .). in aspects of these embodiments , this is easily accomplished by searching a project dependency data structure . by way of illustration , suppose a process wants to find information about a type given its type name . type information is stored in a source file or an object file ( e . g ., a class ). if it exists , a source file is considered the most up - to - date version of type information and will be used instead of the class file . otherwise , the class file can be used . fig3 is an illustration of an exemplary recursive algorithm for searching a project dependency data structure for type information in accordance to various embodiments . although this figure depicts functional steps in a particular order for purposes of illustration , the process is not necessarily limited to any particular order or arrangement of steps . one skilled in the art will appreciate that the various steps portrayed in this figure can be omitted , rearranged , performed in parallel , combined and / or adapted in various ways . the benefit of this process is immediate visibility of source file changes in an external project , like that available for source files internal to the project . similar benefits can be derived by from the effect of a configuration change to the cpl hierarchy itself . that is , the cpl / project hierarchy can be altered ( e . g ., by the user or a process ) and the resulting impact determined on any cpls lower in the hierarchy from the point of change with performance similar to changes in their own source files . the first time this algorithm is invoked , the cpl searched is the project &# 39 ; s . subsequent recursive calls to the algorithm refer to the cpls of other projects . in step 300 , the source files of the cpl are searched for a matching type . if found , the information associated with the type is returned in step 304 . otherwise , a binary - path from the cpl selected in step 306 . in one embodiment , binary - paths are selected in order of dependency . next , an entry from the chosen binary - path is selected in step 308 . if the selected entry is not a directory or a java ® archive ( jar ) file , it is determined in step 312 whether the entry is for a cpl . if so , the algorithm is invoked recursively with the cpl for the entry . if not , the algorithm resumes at step 320 where it is determined if there are any remaining entries to be searched in the chosen binary - path . if the chosen entry is a directory or a jar file , the corresponding directory or file is searched for a matching type in step 314 . if found , the information associated with the type is returned in step 318 . if not , it is determined in step 320 if there are any remaining entries ( i . e ., yet to be searched ) in the chosen binary - path . if so , the process continues at step 308 with the selection of another entry . if not , it is determined in step 322 whether or not there are any remaining binary - paths to search in the current cpl . if so , the algorithm continues at step 306 by choosing another binary - path from the current cpl . if not , the process completes . fig4 is an exemplary illustration of a process for responding to changes in source code . although this figure depicts functional steps in a particular order for purposes of illustration , the process is not necessarily limited to any particular order or arrangement of steps . one skilled in the art will appreciate that the various steps portrayed in this figure can be omitted , rearranged , performed in parallel , combined and / or adapted in various ways . in various embodiments , the software analysis system monitors changes to the code registered for each project . in aspects of these embodiments , changes can be detected in step 400 when modified code is processed by the software analysis system . in one embodiment , processing code includes parsing and analyzing the code according to the syntax and semantics of a programming language and comparing the parsed representation to a previous parsed representation . in step 402 , the software analysis system traverses a project dependency data structure to determine which dependent source code is affected by the change . once the dependent code is identified , the software analysis system can reevaluate the dependent code in step 404 within the context of the modifications and provide notification ( s ) to the associated project in step 406 . a smart editor can then provide relevant information to the software developer , for example by highlighting a syntax error due to the modification of a method signature on a component . fig5 is an exemplary illustration of a system in accordance to various embodiments . although this diagram depicts components as logically separate , such depiction is merely for illustrative purposes . it will be apparent to those skilled in the art that the components portrayed in this figure can be combined or divided into separate software , firmware and / or hardware components . furthermore , it will also be apparent to those skilled in the art that such components , regardless of how they are combined or divided , can execute on the same computing device or can be distributed among different computing devices connected by one or more networks or other suitable communication means . in various embodiments , a compiler framework 506 provides communication between language modules ( 508 - 512 ) for compiling source code and clients of information about the source code , such as ides with “ smart ” editors 504 used by the software developer . the ide allows a software developer to create projects and specify dependencies between projects . the software analysis system 502 utilizes project dependency data structure 500 and causes code to be parsed and analyzed within a project , collects information about that code and presents that information to the ide so the ide can assist the software developer ( e . g ., in editor 504 by adding syntax coloring to the source code , statement completion , etc .). in aspects of these embodiments , the software analysis system maintains a list of locations where internal components may be found for each project . the system allows clients to specify dependencies between projects by inserting references to other software projects within this list . in one embodiment , software developers can specify this information via an ide . the ide can in turn utilize an api to communicate the list to the software analysis system . in one embodiment , a setbinarypaths api method allows the ide ( or other process ) to specify the list of locations where internal components and external projects this project depends on can be found . the ide may call this method passing a list of objects representing locations , which may include directory paths within the project , software libraries within the project or other objects implementing a cpl interface representing external projects . the objects representing external projects may contain similar lists of locations including additional objects representing the projects they depend upon . in one embodiment , the order of the objects provided to the setbinarypaths method is significant — the order defines the order in which the software analysis system searches internal components and external projects to find definitions of components used in the project . in one embodiment , the first definition of a component found in the locations on this list is used by the software analysis system and definitions from subsequent locations are ignored . one embodiment may be implemented using a conventional general purpose or a specialized digital computer or microprocessor ( s ) programmed according to the teachings of the present disclosure , as will be apparent to those skilled in the computer art . appropriate software coding can readily be prepared by skilled programmers based on the teachings of the present disclosure , as will be apparent to those skilled in the software art . the invention may also be implemented by the preparation of integrated circuits or by interconnecting an appropriate network of conventional component circuits , as will be readily apparent to those skilled in the art . one embodiment includes a computer program product which is a storage medium ( media ) having instructions stored thereon / in which can be used to program a computer to perform any of the features presented herein . the storage medium can include , but is not limited to , any type of disk including floppy disks , optical discs , dvd , cd - roms , microdrive , and magneto - optical disks , roms , rams , eproms , eeproms , drams , vrams , flash memory devices , magnetic or optical cards , nanosystems ( including molecular memory ics ), or any type of media or device suitable for storing instructions and / or data . stored on any one of the computer readable medium ( media ), the present invention includes software for controlling both the hardware of the general purpose / specialized computer or microprocessor , and for enabling the computer or microprocessor to interact with a human user or other mechanism utilizing the results of the present invention . such software may include , but is not limited to , device drivers , operating systems , execution environments / containers , and applications . the foregoing description of the preferred embodiments of the present invention has been provided for the purposes of illustration and description . it is not intended to be exhaustive or to limit the invention to the precise forms disclosed . many modifications and variations will be apparent to the practitioner skilled in the art . embodiments were chosen and described in order to best describe the principles of the invention and its practical application , thereby enabling others skilled in the art to understand the invention , the various embodiments and with various modifications that are suited to the particular use contemplated . it is intended that the scope of the invention be defined by the following claims and their equivalents .|3    |

TASK 2 — Task 10: RDD vs DataFrame Comparison

In [4]:
from pyspark.sql import functions as F

df_result = df.groupBy("label").count().orderBy(F.desc("count"))
df_result.show()

+-----+-----+
|label|count|
+-----+-----+
|    6| 5408|
|    7| 5321|
|    0| 3614|
|    1| 3357|
|    8| 2562|
|    2| 2099|
|    5| 1730|
|    4|  705|
|    3|  204|
+-----+-----+
In [5]:
rdd = df.select("label").rdd.map(lambda row: (row["label"], 1))

rdd_result = rdd.reduceByKey(lambda a, b: a + b) \
                .sortBy(lambda x: x[1], ascending=False)

for label, count in rdd_result.take(10):
    print(label, count)

6 5408
7 5321
0 3614
1 3357
8 2562
2 2099
5 1730
4 705
3 204
In [6]:
df_result.explain(True)

== Parsed Logical Plan ==
'Sort ['count DESC NULLS LAST], true
+- Aggregate [label#4L], [label#4L, count(1) AS count#275L]
   +- PythonMapInArrow partial(partition#0)#2, [text#3, label#4L], false
      +- LogicalRDD [partition#0], false

== Analyzed Logical Plan ==
label: bigint, count: bigint
Sort [count#275L DESC NULLS LAST], true
+- Aggregate [label#4L], [label#4L, count(1) AS count#275L]
   +- PythonMapInArrow partial(partition#0)#2, [text#3, label#4L], false
      +- LogicalRDD [partition#0], false

== Optimized Logical Plan ==
Sort [count#275L DESC NULLS LAST], true
+- Aggregate [label#4L], [label#4L, count(1) AS count#275L]
   +- Project [label#4L]
      +- PythonMapInArrow partial(partition#0)#2, [text#3, label#4L], false
         +- LogicalRDD [partition#0], false

== Physical Plan ==
AdaptiveSparkPlan isFinalPlan=false
+- Sort [count#275L DESC NULLS LAST], true, 0
   +- Exchange rangepartitioning(count#275L DESC NULLS LAST, 200), ENSURE_REQUIREMENTS, [plan_id=373]
      +- HashAggregate(keys=[label#4L], functions=[count(1)], output=[label#4L, count#275L])
         +- Exchange hashpartitioning(label#4L, 200), ENSURE_REQUIREMENTS, [plan_id=370]
            +- HashAggregate(keys=[label#4L], functions=[partial_count(1)], output=[label#4L, count#285L])
               +- Project [label#4L]
                  +- PythonMapInArrow partial(partition#0)#2, [text#3, label#4L], false
                     +- Scan ExistingRDD[partition#0]

TASK 3 — Task 16: One-Hot Encoding + VectorAssembler

In [7]:
from pyspark.ml.feature import StringIndexer, Tokenizer, HashingTF, VectorAssembler
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline


indexer = StringIndexer(inputCol="label", outputCol="label_index")


tokenizer = Tokenizer(inputCol="text", outputCol="words")


tf = HashingTF(inputCol="words", outputCol="tf_features", numFeatures=500)


assembler = VectorAssembler(
    inputCols=["tf_features"],
    outputCol="features"
)

pipeline = Pipeline(stages=[indexer, tokenizer, tf, assembler])

model = pipeline.fit(df)
output = model.transform(df)

output.select("label", "label_index", "features").show(5, truncate=False)

+-----+-----------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
only showing top 5 rows

TASK 4 — Task 15: Advanced DF Array / Map Manipulation¶

In [9]:
from pyspark.sql import functions as F
from pyspark.ml.feature import Tokenizer


spark.conf.set("spark.sql.mapKeyDedupPolicy", "LAST_WIN")


tokenizer = Tokenizer(inputCol="text", outputCol="words")
df_tokenized = tokenizer.transform(df)


df_arrays = df_tokenized.select("words")


df_arrays = df_arrays.withColumn(
    "dummy_words", F.array(F.lit("example"), F.lit("word"))
)
df_union = df_arrays.withColumn(
    "combined_words",
    F.array_union(F.col("words"), F.col("dummy_words"))
)


df_except = df_union.withColumn(
    "words_without_example",
    F.array_except(F.col("words"), F.array(F.lit("example")))
)


df_map = df_except.withColumn(
    "word_length_map",
    F.map_from_arrays(
        F.col("words"),
        F.transform(F.col("words"), lambda x: F.length(x))
    )
)


df_final = df_map.withColumn(
    "total_word_count",
    F.size(F.col("words"))
)

df_final.show(5, truncate=False)

Task:3,4,6,7

STEP 1 – Clean working DataFrame

In [10]:
!pip install pyspark datasets --quiet
In [11]:
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, length


patent_df = (
    df
    .dropna(subset=["text", "label"])
    .withColumn("text_length", length(col("text")))
)

patent_df.select("text", "label", "text_length").show(5, truncate=100)

+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+
|                                                                                                text|label|text_length|
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+
|turning now to the drawings , there is shown in fig1 an integrated circuit continuity testing sys...|    6|      20801|
|deployment mechanisms that are configured for use with multi - functional surgical instruments th...|    0|      21127|
|now , first and second embodiments of the present invention will be described below with referenc...|    7|      22150|
|as used herein , “ administration ” of a composition includes any route of administration , inclu...|    0|      28865|
|in accordance with the figures , the mixing device is comprised of a sheath ( 4 ) which surrounds...|    8|      12855|
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+
only showing top 5 rows

TASK 4 – Advanced Text Feature Extraction (REGEX)

Note: It is use for complex patent texts and it creates new feature even this is customise pattern.

In [12]:
from pyspark.sql.functions import regexp_extract


figure_pattern   = r"\bfig(?:ure)?\s*\d+[a-z]?\b"
claim_pattern    = r"\bclaim\s+\d+(\s*-\s*\d+)?\b"
measurement_pat  = r"\b\d+(\.\d+)?\s*(mm|cm|°c|hz|nm|kg)\b"

regex_df = (
    patent_df
    .withColumn("has_figure",     (regexp_extract("text", figure_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
    .withColumn("has_claim_ref",  (regexp_extract("text", claim_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
    .withColumn("has_measurement",(regexp_extract("text", measurement_pat, 0) != "").cast("int"))
)

regex_df.select("has_figure", "has_claim_ref", "has_measurement") \
        .groupBy("has_figure", "has_claim_ref", "has_measurement") \
        .count().show()

+----------+-------------+---------------+-----+
|has_figure|has_claim_ref|has_measurement|count|
+----------+-------------+---------------+-----+
|         0|            1|              1|    4|
|         0|            0|              0| 1102|
|         1|            0|              1| 5118|
|         1|            1|              1|   22|
|         1|            0|              0|17555|
|         1|            1|              0|   37|
|         0|            1|              0|    5|
|         0|            0|              1| 1157|
+----------+-------------+---------------+-----+

TASK 3 – Data Skew Mitigation with Salting

Note: 1. If the label is skewed, then some classes have many patents now, check the label Skew.

In [13]:
from pyspark.sql.functions import desc

label_counts = regex_df.groupBy("label").count().orderBy(desc("count"))
label_counts.show()

+-----+-----+
|label|count|
+-----+-----+
|    6| 5408|
|    7| 5321|
|    0| 3614|
|    1| 3357|
|    8| 2562|
|    2| 2099|
|    5| 1730|
|    4|  705|
|    3|  204|

Note: 2.Aggregation(It's shows cost & skew)

In [14]:
import time
from pyspark.sql.functions import avg

start = time.time()

baseline_agg = (
    regex_df
    .groupBy("label")
    .agg(
        avg("text_length").alias("avg_length"),
        avg("has_measurement").alias("avg_measurement_flag")
    )
)

baseline_agg.show()
print("Baseline groupBy time:", time.time() - start)

+-----+------------------+--------------------+
|label|        avg_length|avg_measurement_flag|
+-----+------------------+--------------------+
|    0|18801.392086330936| 0.33785279468732704|
|    7|18408.452734448412|  0.1871828603645931|
|    6|20887.680658284025| 0.20636094674556213|
|    5|14249.690751445087|  0.1260115606936416|
|    1| 14744.60381292821| 0.19928507596067918|
|    3|14444.666666666666| 0.29901960784313725|
|    8|17257.047228727555|   0.265807962529274|
|    2|26053.952834683183|  0.6060028585040496|
|    4| 15113.39574468085|  0.0950354609929078|
+-----+------------------+--------------------+

Baseline groupBy time: 31.11751365661621

Note: 3. It applies salting to reduce skew

In [15]:
from pyspark.sql.functions import rand, floor, concat_ws

NUM_SALTS = 8

salted_df = (
    regex_df
    .withColumn("salt", floor(rand() * NUM_SALTS))
    .withColumn("label_salted", concat_ws("_", col("label").cast("string"), col("salt")))
)

start = time.time()

salted_agg = (
    salted_df
    .groupBy("label_salted")
    .agg(
        avg("text_length").alias("avg_length_salted")
    )
)

salted_agg.show(20)
print("Salted groupBy time:", time.time() - start)

+------------+------------------+
|label_salted| avg_length_salted|
+------------+------------------+
|         8_6|16580.336633663366|
|         5_4|14850.449781659388|
|         2_7|25314.713768115944|
|         0_5| 18979.93153526971|
|         7_2|18778.132547864505|
|         6_7| 19795.17824773414|
|         5_3|14092.672811059907|
|         3_7|          15090.52|
|         1_5|15229.751842751843|
|         1_4|14897.652173913044|
|         3_4|18373.954545454544|
|         8_1| 18211.54603174603|
|         1_3|14544.812917594654|
|         3_3|          13929.88|
|         2_4|27403.674418604653|
|         8_0|18218.043076923077|
|         6_4|21513.295154185023|
|         6_0|22270.459418070444|
|         6_3|        20721.8375|
|         0_0|19699.673515981736|
+------------+------------------+
only showing top 20 rows

Salted groupBy time: 7.558141231536865

TASK 6 – Persistence Strategy Comparison (RDD, NO pandas)

Discuss about compare memory_only, disk_only, memory_and_disk which is alternative RDD workload

Note: 1. RDD & Dataframe creating

In [16]:
length_rdd = regex_df.select("label", "text_length").rdd.map(
    lambda row: (int(row.label), int(row.text_length))
)

Note: 2. Use for compution which is customise the function like maths, loops etc.

In [17]:
def patent_complexity(pair):
    label, length = pair
    score = 0

    for i in range(6):
        score += (length + i) * (i + 2)
    score += label * 11
    return (label, score)

score_rdd = length_rdd.map(patent_complexity)
In [18]:
from pyspark import StorageLevel
import time

def measure_persistence(rdd_obj, storage_level, label):
    rdd_obj.unpersist(blocking=True)
    rdd_obj.persist(storage_level)

    start = time.time()

    for _ in range(3):
        _ = rdd_obj.reduceByKey(lambda a, b: a + b).count()
    duration = time.time() - start
    print(f"{label}: {duration:.2f} seconds")
    return duration
In [19]:
dur_mem_only = measure_persistence(score_rdd, StorageLevel.MEMORY_ONLY, "MEMORY_ONLY")
dur_disk_only = measure_persistence(score_rdd, StorageLevel.DISK_ONLY, "DISK_ONLY")
dur_mem_disk = measure_persistence(score_rdd, StorageLevel.MEMORY_AND_DISK, "MEMORY_AND_DISK")

score_rdd.unpersist()

MEMORY_ONLY: 8.51 seconds
DISK_ONLY: 6.35 seconds
MEMORY_AND_DISK: 8.98 seconds
Out[19]:
PythonRDD[121] at RDD at PythonRDD.scala:53

TASK 7 – Imbalanced Sampling with RDD

Note:1. It shows how to rebalance the majority and minority label classes and current classes.

In [20]:
label_counts = regex_df.groupBy("label").count().orderBy("label")
label_counts.show()

+-----+-----+
|label|count|
+-----+-----+
|    0| 3614|
|    1| 3357|
|    2| 2099|
|    3|  204|
|    4|  705|
|    5| 1730|
|    6| 5408|
|    7| 5321|
|    8| 2562|
+-----+-----+

Examples:

In [21]:
majority_label = 0
minority_label = 3

Note: 2. this is class to keyed RDD

In [22]:
class_rdd = regex_df.select("label", "text", "text_length").rdd.map(
    lambda row: (int(row.label), (row.text, int(row.text_length)))
)
In [23]:
maj_rdd = class_rdd.filter(lambda x: x[0] == majority_label)
min_rdd = class_rdd.filter(lambda x: x[0] == minority_label)

maj_count = maj_rdd.count()
min_count = min_rdd.count()
print("Before balancing -> majority:", maj_count, "minority:", min_count)

Before balancing -> majority: 3614 minority: 204
In [24]:
sample_fraction = float(min_count) / maj_count

balanced_majority = maj_rdd.sample(False, sample_fraction, seed=123)
balanced_rdd = balanced_majority.union(min_rdd)

print("After undersampling -> majority:",
      balanced_rdd.filter(lambda x: x[0] == majority_label).count())
print("After undersampling -> minority:",
      balanced_rdd.filter(lambda x: x[0] == minority_label).count())

After undersampling -> majority: 219
After undersampling -> minority: 204
In [25]:
factor = int(maj_count / float(min_count)) or 1

oversampled_minority = min_rdd
for _ in range(factor - 1):
    oversampled_minority = oversampled_minority.union(min_rdd)

oversampled_balanced = maj_rdd.union(oversampled_minority)

print("After oversampling -> majority:",
      oversampled_balanced.filter(lambda x: x[0] == majority_label).count())
print("After oversampling -> minority:",
      oversampled_balanced.filter(lambda x: x[0] == minority_label).count())

After oversampling -> majority: 3614
After oversampling -> minority: 3468

Big Data Analytics[CN7031]CRWK 2025-2026

Group-37

Student 2- U3000774_Ravi Sureshbhai Patel

Task:2,12,19,1

In [26]:
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, length, regexp_extract, when

base_df = (
    df
    .dropna(subset=["text", "label"])
    .withColumn("text_length", length(col("text")))
)

figure_pattern   = r"\bfig(?:ure)?\s*\d+[a-z]?\b"
claim_pattern    = r"\bclaim\s+\d+(\s*-\s*\d+)?\b"

feature_df = (
    base_df
    .withColumn("has_figure", (regexp_extract("text", figure_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
    .withColumn("has_claim",  (regexp_extract("text", claim_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
)

feature_df.select("text", "label", "text_length", "has_figure", "has_claim") \
          .show(5, truncate=100)

+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+----------+---------+
|                                                                                                text|label|text_length|has_figure|has_claim|
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+----------+---------+
|turning now to the drawings , there is shown in fig1 an integrated circuit continuity testing sys...|    6|      20801|         1|        0|
|deployment mechanisms that are configured for use with multi - functional surgical instruments th...|    0|      21127|         1|        0|
|now , first and second embodiments of the present invention will be described below with referenc...|    7|      22150|         1|        0|
|as used herein , “ administration ” of a composition includes any route of administration , inclu...|    0|      28865|         1|        0|
|in accordance with the figures , the mixing device is comprised of a sheath ( 4 ) which surrounds...|    8|      12855|         1|        0|
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+----------+---------+
only showing top 5 rows

TASK 2 – Broadcast Join Implementation & Justification

In [27]:
from pyspark.sql import Row

label_info = [
    Row(label=0, label_desc="Mechanical / Devices"),
    Row(label=1, label_desc="Chemistry / Materials"),
    Row(label=2, label_desc="Electrical Systems"),
    Row(label=3, label_desc="Computer / Software"),
    Row(label=4, label_desc="Medical / Bio"),
    Row(label=5, label_desc="Energy / Power"),
    Row(label=6, label_desc="Communication / Networks"),
    Row(label=7, label_desc="Manufacturing / Process"),
    Row(label=8, label_desc="Other / Misc")
]

lookup_df = spark.createDataFrame(label_info)
lookup_df.show()

left_df = feature_df.select("label", "text_length", "has_figure", "has_claim")

start = time.time()

normal_join = (
    left_df
    .join(lookup_df, on="label", how="inner")
)

normal_join.count()
normal_time = time.time() - start
print("Normal join time:", normal_time)

broadcast_join = (
    left_df
    .join(broadcast(lookup_df), on="label", how="inner")
)

broadcast_join.count()
broadcast_time = time.time() - start
print("Broadcast join time:", broadcast_time)

TASK 12 – Optimised Data Bucketing

In [32]:
import os
import shutil
import time

from pyspark.sql.functions import col, length, regexp_extract, avg
from pyspark.sql import Row


try:
    import pyspark_huggingface

except ImportError:
    pass



figure_pattern = r"\bfig(?:ure)?\s*\d+[a-z]?\b"
claim_pattern  = r"\bclaim\s+\d+(\s*-\s*\d+)?\b"

feature_df = (
    df
    .dropna(subset=["text", "label"])
    .withColumn("text_length", length(col("text")))
    .withColumn("has_figure", (regexp_extract("text", figure_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
    .withColumn("has_claim",  (regexp_extract("text", claim_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
)

print("Sample of feature_df:")
feature_df.select("text", "label", "text_length", "has_figure", "has_claim") \
          .show(5, truncate=100)


non_bucket_path = "patent_non_bucketed"

if os.path.exists(non_bucket_path):
    shutil.rmtree(non_bucket_path)


(
    feature_df
    .write
    .mode("overwrite")
    .parquet(non_bucket_path)
)

print(f"Non-bucketed Parquet written to: {non_bucket_path}")


spark.sql("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS patent_bucketed_tbl")

(
    feature_df
    .write
    .bucketBy(8, "label")
    .sortBy("label")
    .mode("overwrite")
    .saveAsTable("patent_bucketed_tbl")
)

print("Bucketed table 'patent_bucketed_tbl' created.")


non_bucketed_df = spark.read.parquet(non_bucket_path)
bucketed_df     = spark.table("patent_bucketed_tbl")

print("Non-bucketed DF rows:", non_bucketed_df.count())
print("Bucketed DF rows:    ", bucketed_df.count())


def benchmark_filter_agg(df_in, label_value, label_name="(no label)"):
    start = time.time()
    (
        df_in
        .filter(col("label") == label_value)
        .groupBy("label")
        .agg(
            avg("text_length").alias("avg_len"),
            avg("has_figure").alias("avg_has_figure")
        )
        .collect()
    )
    duration = time.time() - start
    print(f"{label_name} -> filter+agg time: {duration:.4f} seconds")
    return duration


example_label = 0

print("\n=== Benchmark: filter + groupBy on label ===")
t_non_bucket = benchmark_filter_agg(non_bucketed_df, example_label, "Non-bucketed")
t_bucketed   = benchmark_filter_agg(bucketed_df,     example_label, "Bucketed")

print("\nFilter+agg summary:")
print(f"Non-bucketed time: {t_non_bucket:.4f} seconds")
print(f"Bucketed time:     {t_bucketed:.4f} seconds")


label_info = [
    Row(label=0, label_desc="Mechanical"),
    Row(label=1, label_desc="Chemistry"),
    Row(label=2, label_desc="Electrical"),
    Row(label=3, label_desc="Software"),
    Row(label=4, label_desc="Medical"),
    Row(label=5, label_desc="Energy"),
    Row(label=6, label_desc="Comms"),
    Row(label=7, label_desc="Process"),
    Row(label=8, label_desc="Other"),
]

lookup_df = spark.createDataFrame(label_info)

def benchmark_join(df_in, label_value, label_name="(no label)"):
    start = time.time()
    (
        df_in
        .filter(col("label") == label_value)
        .join(lookup_df, on="label", how="inner")
        .count()
    )
    duration = time.time() - start
    print(f"{label_name} join -> time: {duration:.4f} seconds")
    return duration

print("\n=== Benchmark: join with small lookup table ===")
tj_non_bucket = benchmark_join(non_bucketed_df, example_label, "Non-bucketed")
tj_bucketed   = benchmark_join(bucketed_df,     example_label, "Bucketed")

print("\nJoin summary:")
print(f"Non-bucketed join time: {tj_non_bucket:.4f} seconds")
print(f"Bucketed join time:     {tj_bucketed:.4f} seconds")

Sample of feature_df:

|                                                                                                text|label|text_length|has_figure|has_claim|
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+----------+---------+

|deployment mechanisms that are configured for use with multi - functional surgical instruments th...|    0|      21127|         1|        0|
|now , first and second embodiments of the present invention will be described below with referenc...|    7|      22150|         1|        0|
|as used herein , “ administration ” of a composition includes any route of administration , inclu...|    0|      28865|         1|        0|
|in accordance with the figures , the mixing device is comprised of a sheath ( 4 ) which surrounds...|    8|      12855|         1|        0|

TASK 19 – Parquet vs ORC Columnar Format Comparison

In [33]:
parquet_fmt_path = "patent_parquet_snappy"
orc_fmt_path     = "patent_orc_snappy"

for p in [parquet_fmt_path, orc_fmt_path]:
    if os.path.exists(p):
        shutil.rmtree(p)

(
    feature_df
    .write
    .mode("overwrite")
    .option("compression", "snappy")
    .parquet(parquet_fmt_path)
)

(
    feature_df
    .write
    .mode("overwrite")
    .option("compression", "snappy")
    .orc(orc_fmt_path)
)
In [34]:
def folder_size_mb(path):
    total = 0
    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(path):
        for f in files:
            total += os.path.getsize(os.path.join(root, f))
    return total / (1024**2)

parquet_size = folder_size_mb(parquet_fmt_path)
orc_size     = folder_size_mb(orc_fmt_path)

print(f"Parquet size (MB): {parquet_size:.2f}")
print(f"ORC size (MB):     {orc_size:.2f}")

Parquet size (MB): 186.94
ORC size (MB):     186.84
In [35]:
import time

def benchmark_format(read_func, path, label):
    start = time.time()
    df_in = read_func(path).select("label", "text_length")
    (
        df_in
        .filter(col("label") == label)
        .groupBy("label")
        .agg(avg("text_length").alias("avg_len"))
        .collect()
    )
    return time.time() - start

test_label = 0

parquet_time = benchmark_format(spark.read.parquet, parquet_fmt_path, test_label)
orc_time     = benchmark_format(spark.read.orc, orc_fmt_path, test_label)

print("Parquet read+agg time:", parquet_time)
print("ORC read+agg time:   ", orc_time)

Parquet read+agg time: 0.6370522975921631
ORC read+agg time:    0.8707101345062256

TASK 1 – Hierarchical Data Parsing & Flattening¶

In [36]:
from pyspark.sql.functions import (
    col,
    length,
    regexp_extract,
    split,
    explode,
    struct,
    array,
    lit,
    monotonically_increasing_id
)


try:
    import pyspark_huggingface

except ImportError:
    pass



figure_pattern = r"\bfig(?:ure)?\s*\d+[a-z]?\b"
claim_pattern  = r"\bclaim\s+\d+(\s*-\s*\d+)?\b"

base_df = (
    df
    .dropna(subset=["text", "label"])
    .withColumn("text_length", length(col("text")))
    .withColumn("has_figure", (regexp_extract("text", figure_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
    .withColumn("has_claim",  (regexp_extract("text", claim_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
)

print("=== Sample base_df ===")
base_df.select("label", "text_length", "has_figure", "has_claim", "text") \
       .show(3, truncate=100)



nested_df = (
    base_df
    .withColumn("patent_id", monotonically_increasing_id())

    .withColumn("raw_sentences", split(col("text"), r"\. "))

    .withColumn(
        "sections",
        array(
            struct(lit("intro").alias("section_name"),
                  col("raw_sentences").getItem(0).alias("content")),
            struct(lit("middle").alias("section_name"),
                  col("raw_sentences").getItem(1).alias("content")),
            struct(lit("conclusion").alias("section_name"),
                  col("raw_sentences").getItem(2).alias("content"))
        )
    )

    .select(
        struct(
            col("patent_id").alias("patent_id"),
            struct(
                col("label").alias("label"),
                col("text_length").alias("text_length")
            ).alias("metadata"),
            struct(
                col("has_figure").alias("has_figure"),
                col("has_claim").alias("has_claim")
            ).alias("flags"),
            col("sections").alias("sections")
        ).alias("patent_nested")
    )
)

print("\n=== Nested schema (patent_nested) ===")
nested_df.printSchema()

print("\n=== Example of nested rows ===")
nested_df.show(3, truncate=False)


flattened_df = (
    nested_df
    .select(
        col("patent_nested.patent_id").alias("patent_id"),
        col("patent_nested.metadata.label").alias("label"),
        col("patent_nested.metadata.text_length").alias("text_length"),
        col("patent_nested.flags.has_figure").alias("has_figure"),
        col("patent_nested.flags.has_claim").alias("has_claim"),
        explode(col("patent_nested.sections")).alias("section")
    )
    .select(
        "patent_id",
        "label",
        "text_length",
        "has_figure",
        "has_claim",
        col("section.section_name").alias("section_name"),
        col("section.content").alias("section_content")
    )
)

print("\n=== Flattened schema ===")
flattened_df.printSchema()

print("\n=== Sample flattened data (one row per section) ===")
flattened_df.show(10, truncate=80)

=== Sample base_df ===
+-----+-----------+----------+---------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|label|text_length|has_figure|has_claim|                                                                                                text|
+-----+-----------+----------+---------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|    6|      20801|         1|        0|turning now to the drawings , there is shown in fig1 an integrated circuit continuity testing sys...|
|    0|      21127|         1|        0|deployment mechanisms that are configured for use with multi - functional surgical instruments th...|
|    7|      22150|         1|        0|now , first and second embodiments of the present invention will be described below with referenc...|
+-----+-----------+----------+---------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
only showing top 3 rows


=== Nested schema (patent_nested) ===
root
 |-- patent_nested: struct (nullable = false)
 |    |-- patent_id: long (nullable = false)
 |    |-- metadata: struct (nullable = false)
 |    |    |-- label: long (nullable = true)
 |    |    |-- text_length: integer (nullable = true)
 |    |-- flags: struct (nullable = false)
 |    |    |-- has_figure: integer (nullable = true)
 |    |    |-- has_claim: integer (nullable = true)
 |    |-- sections: array (nullable = false)
 |    |    |-- element: struct (containsNull = false)
 |    |    |    |-- section_name: string (nullable = false)
 |    |    |    |-- content: string (nullable = true)


=== Example of nested rows ===

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|{0, {6, 20801}, {1, 0}, [{intro, turning now to the drawings , there is shown in fig1 an integrated circuit continuity testing system in which a specimen or circuit configuration 16 is mounted on a fixture 18 operable to vibrate the specimen under controlled conditions , e }, {middle, g }, {conclusion, sinusoidally , randomly , or a combination of the two }]}                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|{1, {0, 21127}, {1, 0}, [{intro, deployment mechanisms that are configured for use with multi - functional surgical instruments that are operable in bipolar and / or monopolar modes of operation may prove useful in the surgical arena , and such deployment mechanisms are described herein }, {middle, specifically , the deployment mechanisms described herein include one or more linkage configurations that , when actuated , move a monopolar electrode of the electrosurgical forceps from a retracted configuration to a deployed configuration to electrosurgically treat tissue }, {conclusion, fig1 - 4 illustrate a forceps 10 that includes a deployment mechanism 28 in accordance with an embodiment of the present disclosure }]}|
|{2, {7, 22150}, {1, 0}, [{intro, now , first and second embodiments of the present invention will be described below with reference to the accompanying drawings }, {middle, in the following description of the drawings in the first and second embodiment , identical or similar constituents are designated by identical or similar reference numerals }, {conclusion, fig1 is a view showing a wireless transceiver device according to a first embodiment of the present invention }]}                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

 |-- section_content: string (nullable = true)

from pyspark.sql.functions import col, length, regexp_extract

!pip install pyspark_huggingface --quiet
import pyspark_huggingface
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession


spark = SparkSession.builder \
        .appName('pyspark_huggingface') \
        .config("spark.sql.warehouse.dir", "/content/spark-warehouse") \
        .getOrCreate()

df = spark.read.format('huggingface').load('ccdv/patent-classification')

base_df = (
    df
    .dropna(subset=["text", "label"])
    .withColumn("text_length", length(col("text")))
)

figure_pattern   = r"\bfig(?:ure)?\s*\d+[a-z]?\b"
claim_pattern    = r"\bclaim\s+\d+(\s*-\s*\d+)?\b"

feature_df = (
    base_df
    .withColumn("has_figure", (regexp_extract("text", figure_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
    .withColumn("has_claim",  (regexp_extract("text", claim_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
)

feature_df.select("text", "label", "text_length", "has_figure", "has_claim") \
          .show(5, truncate=100)

+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+----------+---------+
|                                                                                                text|label|text_length|has_figure|has_claim|

|deployment mechanisms that are configured for use with multi - functional surgical instruments th...|    0|      21127|         1|        0|
|now , first and second embodiments of the present invention will be described below with referenc...|    7|      22150|         1|        0|
|as used herein , “ administration ” of a composition includes any route of administration , inclu...|    0|      28865|         1|        0|

In [38]:
from pyspark.sql.window import Window
from pyspark.sql.functions import avg, row_number, dense_rank
In [39]:
w_label_order = Window.partitionBy("label").orderBy(col("text_length").asc())

w_label = Window.partitionBy("label")
In [40]:
window_df = (
    feature_df
    .withColumn(
        "moving_avg_length",
        avg("text_length").over(w_label_order.rowsBetween(Window.unboundedPreceding, 0))
    )
    .withColumn(
        "length_rank_in_label",
        dense_rank().over(w_label_order)
    )
)

window_df.select(
    "label", "text_length", "moving_avg_length", "length_rank_in_label"
).show(10, truncate=False)

|label|text_length|moving_avg_length |length_rank_in_label|

|0    |2795       |2345.0            |3                   |

== Physical Plan ==
AdaptiveSparkPlan (8)
+- Window (7)
   +- Sort (6)
      +- Exchange (5)
         +- Project (4)
            +- Filter (3)
               +- PythonMapInArrow (2)
                  +- Scan ExistingRDD (1)


(1) Scan ExistingRDD
Output [1]: [partition#1369]
Arguments: [partition#1369], MapPartitionsRDD[418] at applySchemaToPythonRDD at NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:0, ExistingRDD, UnknownPartitioning(0)

(2) PythonMapInArrow
Input [1]: [partition#1369]
Arguments: partial(partition#1369)#1371, [text#1372, label#1373L], false

(3) Filter
Input [2]: [text#1372, label#1373L]
Condition : atleastnnonnulls(2, text#1372, label#1373L)

(4) Project
Output [5]: [text#1372, label#1373L, length(text#1372) AS text_length#1378, cast(NOT (regexp_extract(text#1372, \bfig(?:ure)?\s*\d+[a-z]?\b, 0) = ) as int) AS has_figure#1382, cast(NOT (regexp_extract(text#1372, \bclaim\s+\d+(\s*-\s*\d+)?\b, 0) = ) as int) AS has_claim#1387]
Input [2]: [text#1372, label#1373L]

(5) Exchange
Input [5]: [text#1372, label#1373L, text_length#1378, has_figure#1382, has_claim#1387]
Arguments: hashpartitioning(label#1373L, 200), ENSURE_REQUIREMENTS, [plan_id=2728]

(6) Sort
Input [5]: [text#1372, label#1373L, text_length#1378, has_figure#1382, has_claim#1387]
Arguments: [label#1373L ASC NULLS FIRST, text_length#1378 ASC NULLS FIRST], false, 0

(7) Window
Input [5]: [text#1372, label#1373L, text_length#1378, has_figure#1382, has_claim#1387]
Arguments: [avg(text_length#1378) windowspecdefinition(label#1373L, text_length#1378 ASC NULLS FIRST, specifiedwindowframe(RowFrame, unboundedpreceding$(), currentrow$())) AS moving_avg_length#1420, dense_rank(text_length#1378) windowspecdefinition(label#1373L, text_length#1378 ASC NULLS FIRST, specifiedwindowframe(RowFrame, unboundedpreceding$(), currentrow$())) AS length_rank_in_label#1429], [label#1373L], [text_length#1378 ASC NULLS FIRST]

(8) AdaptiveSparkPlan
Output [7]: [text#1372, label#1373L, text_length#1378, has_figure#1382, has_claim#1387, moving_avg_length#1420, length_rank_in_label#1429]

    spark_max("label").alias("max_label")
)

    (col("text_length") > 0) &
    (col("label") >= 0) &
    (col("label") <= 8)
)

In [45]:
spark.sparkContext.setCheckpointDir("checkpoints")
In [46]:
current_df = dq_df
threshold = 100

for step in range(3):
    print(f"Iteration {step} with threshold {threshold}")

    current_df = current_df.filter(col("text_length") > threshold)

    current_df = current_df.checkpoint(eager=True)

    print("Row count after step", step, ":", current_df.count())

    threshold += 200

Iteration 0 with threshold 100
Row count after step 0 : 25000
Iteration 1 with threshold 300
Row count after step 1 : 25000
Iteration 2 with threshold 500
Row count after step 2 : 25000

TASK 20 – Shuffling Optimization (spark.sql.shuffle.partitions)

In [51]:
import time
from pyspark.sql.functions import avg, count

def run_wide_aggregation(num_partitions):
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.shuffle.partitions", str(num_partitions))
    print(f"\n=== Running with spark.sql.shuffle.partitions = {num_partitions} ===")

    start = time.time()

    agg_df = (
        feature_df
        .groupBy("label")
        .agg(
            count("*").alias("n_rows"),
            avg("text_length").alias("avg_len"),
            avg("has_figure").alias("avg_has_figure")
        )
    )

    agg_df.collect()

    duration = time.time() - start
    print(f"Execution time: {duration:.2f} seconds")

    agg_df.explain(mode="simple")

    return duration
In [50]:
times = {}

for p in [8, 64, 256]:
    times[p] = run_wide_aggregation(p)

print("\nSummary of timings:")
for p, t in times.items():
    print(f"Partitions={p}: {t:.2f} seconds")

In [53]:
import time
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, length, regexp_extract, avg, count
from pyspark.sql import Row


try:
    import pyspark_huggingface

except ImportError:
    pass


figure_pattern   = r"\bfig(?:ure)?\s*\d+[a-z]?\b"
claim_pattern    = r"\bclaim\s+\d+(\s*-\s*\d+)?\b"

tune_df = (
    df
    .dropna(subset=["text", "label"])
    .withColumn("text_length", length(col("text")))
    .withColumn("has_figure", (regexp_extract("text", figure_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
    .withColumn("has_claim",  (regexp_extract("text", claim_pattern, 0) != "").cast("int"))
)

print("Sample rows from tune_df:")
tune_df.select("label", "text_length", "has_figure", "has_claim").show(5, truncate=80)


label_info = [
    Row(label=0, label_desc="Mechanical"),
    Row(label=1, label_desc="Chemistry"),
    Row(label=2, label_desc="Electrical"),
    Row(label=3, label_desc="Software"),
    Row(label=4, label_desc="Medical"),
    Row(label=5, label_desc="Energy"),
    Row(label=6, label_desc="Comms"),
    Row(label=7, label_desc="Process"),
    Row(label=8, label_desc="Other"),
]

lookup_df = spark.createDataFrame(label_info)

print("Lookup table:")
lookup_df.show()


def run_config_test(
    config_name: str,
    shuffle_partitions: int,
    df_repartition: int
):
    """
    Runs a heavy job (repartition + join + groupBy + agg)
    under a specific Spark configuration and reports runtime.
    """
    print(f"\n==============================")
    print(f"Running config: {config_name}")
    print(f"  spark.sql.shuffle.partitions = {shuffle_partitions}")
    print(f"  DataFrame.repartition        = {df_repartition}")
    print(f"==============================")

    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.shuffle.partitions", str(shuffle_partitions))

    work_df = tune_df.repartition(df_repartition, "label")

    start = time.time()

    result_df = (
        work_df
        .join(lookup_df, on="label", how="inner")
        .groupBy("label", "label_desc")
        .agg(
            count("*").alias("n_rows"),
            avg("text_length").alias("avg_len"),
            avg("has_figure").alias("avg_has_figure"),
            avg("has_claim").alias("avg_has_claim"),
        )
        .orderBy("label")
    )

    result = result_df.collect()

    duration = time.time() - start

    print(f"Config '{config_name}' finished in {duration:.4f} seconds")
    print("Sample aggregated result:")
    for row in result[:5]:
        print(row)

    return duration



timings = {}


timings["Config_A"] = run_config_test(
    config_name="Config_A (shuffle=32, repartition=16)",
    shuffle_partitions=32,
    df_repartition=16
)


timings["Config_B"] = run_config_test(
    config_name="Config_B (shuffle=256, repartition=64)",
    shuffle_partitions=256,
    df_repartition=64
)

print("\n===== Summary of Task 8 timings =====")
for name, t in timings.items():
    print(f"{name}: {t:.4f} seconds")

print("\nCurrent spark.sql.shuffle.partitions:", spark.conf.get("spark.sql.shuffle.partitions"))
